dict.string: Used to open Terminal so you can run `claude /login` when adding an account.
Copyright © 2026 Thanh Ngô. Licensed under MIT.
Claude Bar 11.13 — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts

MCP write-tool approval prompts (Slack post, Google Sheets write, GitHub PR comment, and the other gated tools) now reliably surface even when you're focused on Claude Code. The banner notification uses .timeSensitive interruption so Focus / Do Not Disturb modes no longer suppress it inside the 60-second approval window, and the auto-opened popover lifts above any Settings or Daily panel that might otherwise have hidden it.

Fixed MCP gate prompts silently timing out after 60s when DND / Focus modes suppressed the banner — Claude Code would receive "user_cancelled: gate timed out" with no on-screen indication anything happened. Fixed the gate popover auto-opening behind Settings / Daily windows when those panels were already on screen, so the approve / cancel buttons sat invisibly underneath them until the 60s window expired.

Each web-poll cycle still loads a hidden WKWebView reload of claude.ai/settings/usage per linked account and waits for the React SPA to hydrate (typically 1–3s, hard-capped at 12s). At the 120s / 60s cadence this fits comfortably for 2–3 accounts; users with many more web-linked accounts may want to disable web-link on the less-used ones. The reset-clause regex doesn't yet parse every locale's date format (e.g., "Resets Fri 7:00 PM"). When parsing fails the widget falls back to a future placeholder (now + 5h for the session bar, now + 7d for the weekly bar) so the percentage still displays — the countdown will be approximate until the next successful parse. The menu-bar popover still dismisses when the Rename window opens (macOS menu-bar app behaviour, not preventable without giving up the dropdown style). The rename itself goes through on Save; reopen the menu to see the new name. Existing v11.x Google tokens are still missing the `spreadsheets` scope shipped in 11.3. The cb_gsheets_* tools return Google "insufficient permission" until you Disconnect + Reconnect the Google connector — that mints a fresh token with the new scope set. Drive / Calendar / Gmail tools keep working on the old scope until you do.
